title: neltuma chilensis var. catamarcana
side_effects: Neltuma chilensis var. catamarcana can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of saponins. In rare cases, excessive consumption can lead to more severe complications, such as kidney damage and electrolyte imbalances. Additionally, the plant's toxic compounds may interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications, exacerbating their side effects. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems in sensitive individuals.
